original_hypothesis.title: LiquidProxy.harvest() Claims Rewards N Times Redundantly, Risking DoS
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: LiquidProxy.harvest() loops over _consensusAddrs.length and calls claimRewards(_consensusAddrs) — passing the FULL array — on EACH iteration. This means rewards are claimed N times for the same set of validators. The first call claims all rewards; subsequent calls attempt to claim zero. If the Ronin staking contract reverts on zero-reward claims, harvest becomes impossible for arrays with length > 1. Additionally, the function uses address(this).balance as claimed amount, which includes any pre-existing native RON from prior operations or external sends to the proxy's open receive().
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The loop iterates `_consensusAddrs.length` times, but each iteration passes the entire `_consensusAddrs` array to claimRewards, not just `_consensusAddrs[i]`. This is a clear code defect — the intent was likely to call once or to call per-address.

proof_of_signal.pattern: for (i = 0; i < _consensusAddrs.length; i++) { claimRewards(_consensusAddrs); } — passes full array in loop

preconditions: Ronin staking contract behavior on duplicate/zero-reward claims must be verified

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: Full array passed in loop instead of single element or single call
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: N redundant claimRewards calls; also address(this).balance includes pre-existing RON
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No balance delta tracking; no single-call pattern

analysis_questions: Does Ronin staking contract revert on claimRewards with zero claimable rewards?
If so, harvest is permanently DoS'd for multi-validator arrays
How much gas is wasted on redundant calls?
validation_requirements: Test claimRewards behavior with zero rewards (does it revert?)
Measure gas cost with multiple validators
Test harvest with pre-existing RON in proxy via open receive()

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: If Ronin staking reverts on zero-reward claims, any harvest call with >1 consensus address fails after the first iteration claims everything. This blocks all reward collection. Even if it doesn't revert, N-1 redundant calls waste gas and the balance-based accounting is inaccurate.
prerequisites: Multiple consensus addresses passed to harvest
Ronin staking contract behavior on duplicate claims

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Complete reward harvesting failure if staking contract reverts on empty claims; otherwise gas waste and accounting inaccuracy
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All protocol users — rewards cannot be harvested
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Workaround: call harvest with single-element arrays (wastes gas)

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test for multi-validator harvest behavior

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Easily detectable via failed harvest transactions

validation_result.mechanism: Redundant loop passing full array instead of single element
validation_result.impact_summary: LiquidProxy.harvest() calls claimRewards with the FULL consensus address array N times (once per element), resulting in N-1 redundant calls. If the Ronin staking contract reverts on zero-reward claims, harvest is DoS'd for multi-validator arrays. Additionally, address(this).balance includes pre-existing RON.
validation_result.detailed_analysis:
The LiquidProxy.harvest() function contains a clear code defect. It iterates over _consensusAddrs.length but passes the entire _consensusAddrs array to claimRewards on each iteration. The loop variable `i` is never used inside the loop body. The first iteration claims all rewards for all validators in the array. Subsequent iterations attempt to claim rewards that have already been claimed (zero rewards). This is redundant at best and causes a DoS at worst if the Ronin staking contract reverts when there are no rewards to claim.

Additionally, after the loop, the function uses address(this).balance as the claimed amount. Since LiquidProxy has an open receive() function, any native RON sent to the proxy by external parties or left over from prior operations would be included in the claimed amount, inflating the harvested rewards and consequently the operator fee calculation in LiquidRon.harvest().
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The defect is directly visible in LiquidProxy.sol. The for loop at line 30 iterates _consensusAddrs.length times, but line 31 passes the full _consensusAddrs array (not _consensusAddrs[i]) to claimRewards. The loop variable i is unused. This is a textbook bug where the developer likely intended either (a) a single call outside a loop, or (b) per-element calls within a loop. Compare with delegateAmount() in the same contract which correctly uses _consensusAddrs[i] inside its loop.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: The loop passes the FULL _consensusAddrs array to claimRewards on each iteration instead of a single element. The loop variable `i` is never referenced in the loop body. This results in N redundant calls. Compare with delegateAmount() which correctly accesses _consensusAddrs[i] and _amounts[i] per iteration.

This is the CORRECT pattern in the same contract - each iteration uses _amounts[i] and _consensusAddrs[i]. The harvest function should follow a similar pattern or simply call claimRewards once without a loop.

calling_sequence: 3. LiquidProxy.harvest() enters loop: for (i = 0; i < _consensusAddrs.length; i++)
4. Each iteration calls IRoninValidator(roninStaking).claimRewards(_consensusAddrs) — passing FULL array
5. First call claims all rewards for all validators; subsequent N-1 calls are redundant
6. After loop: claimedAmount = address(this).balance (includes any pre-existing RON)
7. _depositRONTo(vault, claimedAmount) wraps and sends all RON to vault
8. Back in LiquidRon: operatorFeeAmount += (harvestedAmount * operatorFee) / BIPS
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The critical defect is in LiquidProxy.sol lines 30-32. The for loop iterates _consensusAddrs.length times but line 31 passes the entire _consensusAddrs array to claimRewards() rather than _consensusAddrs[i]. The loop variable i is declared in the for statement but never used in the loop body. This means claimRewards is called N times with the same arguments. After the first call claims all rewards, subsequent calls attempt to claim zero. If the Ronin staking contract reverts on zero-reward claims, the entire harvest transaction reverts for any array with length > 1. Even if it doesn't revert, N-1 redundant external calls waste gas proportional to the number of validators.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: Deployment script LiquidRon_saigon.s.sol creates 5 consensus addresses and delegates to all 5 via both proxies. This confirms multi-validator arrays are the expected usage pattern, making the bug highly relevant in production.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation:
Scenario 1 (DoS): Operator calls harvest(0, [addr1, addr2, addr3, addr4, addr5]) with 5 consensus addresses (as shown in deployment script). The first claimRewards call claims all rewards. The second through fifth calls attempt to claim zero rewards. If Ronin staking reverts on zero claims, the transaction fails entirely and rewards cannot be harvested through the normal path. Workaround: call harvest with single-element arrays, requiring 5 separate transactions.

Scenario 2 (Balance inflation): An attacker sends 1 RON directly to the LiquidProxy via its open receive(). When harvest is called, address(this).balance includes this 1 RON. The 1 RON is counted as harvested rewards and operator fee is charged on it: operatorFeeAmount increases by (1e18 * operatorFee / BIPS). This inflates the fee at depositors' expense.
validation_result.impact_assessment: HIGH - The redundant loop is a clear code defect. If the Ronin staking contract reverts on empty claims, harvest() becomes non-functional for multi-validator scenarios (which is the standard deployment pattern with 5 validators per the deployment script). Even without reversion, N-1 redundant external calls waste substantial gas. The address(this).balance usage compounds the issue by allowing manipulation of harvested amounts through the open receive() function. The deployment script confirms 5 consensus addresses are used, meaning every harvest call would make 5 redundant claimRewards calls instead of 1.
recommended_mitigations: Remove the loop and call claimRewards once: `IRoninValidator(roninStaking).claimRewards(_consensusAddrs);` without any for loop

Track balance delta instead of using address(this).balance: record `uint256 balanceBefore = address(this).balance;` before the claim, then `uint256 claimedAmount = address(this).balance - balanceBefore;` after
Alternatively, if per-element claims are intended, use `claimRewards` with a single-element array: create a temporary array containing only `_consensusAddrs[i]`
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy LiquidRon with mock Ronin staking contract. 2. Deploy two staking proxies. 3. Delegate to 3+ validators. 4. Accumulate rewards. 5. Call harvest with all 3 validator addresses. 6. Observe that claimRewards is called 3 times with the same full array via event logs or gas tracing. 7. Test with a mock staking contract that reverts on zero-reward claims to demonstrate DoS. 8. Send RON directly to proxy, then harvest, and verify the sent RON is included in harvestedAmount.
uncertainties: Whether the Ronin staking contract reverts or returns gracefully on zero-reward claimRewards calls — this determines if the bug causes a full DoS or just gas waste
The exact gas cost overhead per redundant claimRewards call on the Ronin network
additional_testing_needed: Test claimRewards behavior on the Ronin staking contract (0x9C245671791834daf3885533D24dce516B763B28 on Saigon testnet) when called with zero claimable rewards
Measure gas consumption of harvest with varying numbers of consensus addresses
Test sending RON to proxy via receive() and verify it inflates harvestedAmount
